Diversification can reduce the risk specific to individual stocks
Overall market risk can be completely eliminated through diversification alone

prompt: Which best describes the effect of 'dollar-cost averaging (DCA)'?

choices.text: It spreads out the timing of purchases, reducing the volatility of the average purchase price
It always lets you buy at the lowest point

prompt: Arrange the general priorities of personal financial management in the commonly recommended order.

choices.text: Increasing the allocation to additional risk assets

prompt: Which statement about index funds (index-tracking funds) is correct?

choices.text: It tracks a specific index as is, and its fees are generally low
A manager actively selects stocks, so its fees are high
It holds only a single stock

prompt: Which statement about the difference between active funds and passive (index) funds is correct?

choices.text: Active funds aim for excess returns and generally have higher management fees
Passive funds always guarantee higher returns
Passive funds are managed independently of any index

prompt: Select all of the factors that favor growing the 'compounding effect'.

choices.text: Leakage from fees and taxes due to frequent trading

prompt: Which best describes the meaning of 'asset allocation'?

choices.text: Deciding the investment weight for each asset class such as stocks, bonds, and cash

prompt: Which statement about portfolio 'rebalancing' is correct?

choices.text: Periodically adjusting asset weights that have drifted from their targets back to the original levels
Unconditionally buying more of the assets that have gained

prompt: Which best describes the main purpose of an 'emergency fund'?

choices.text: Securing immediately available cash to prepare for unexpected expenses

prompt: Select all of the factors that tend to erode long-term investment performance.

choices.text: High management fees and transaction costs

prompt: Which of the following is most closely classified as a 'safe asset'?

choices.text: Government bonds of a highly creditworthy country
An individual growth stock with high volatility

choices.text: Because the price movements of the two assets are not always perfectly identical

prompt: Arrange the reasonable steps for constructing a long-term investment portfolio in order.

choices.text: Define investment goals and risk tolerance
Decide the target weight for each asset class (asset allocation)
Select and buy products (ETFs, funds, etc.) suited to each asset class

prompt: Which comparison of simple interest and compound interest is NOT correct?

choices.text: Simple interest accrues only on the principal
Compound interest accrues on interest as well
The longer the period, the more advantageous compound interest is over simple interest

prompt: What investment principle does the phrase 'high risk high return, low risk low return' refer to?

choices.text: Risk and expected return generally have a proportional trade-off relationship
The higher the risk, the more returns are guaranteed
The lower the risk, the higher the returns
Risk and return have no relationship at all

prompt: Which statement about 'leveraged (borrowed) investing' is correct?

choices.text: Not only returns but also losses can be amplified

It is always a safe investment method

prompt: Select all that are recommended as sound personal financial habits.

choices.text: Spending less than you earn and consistently saving and investing
Concentrating your entire net worth in a single asset
